raw.objective: “to determine the factors that influence valproate clearance (CL) in Mexican epileptic pediatric patients using a mixed-effect model and sparse data of serum concentrations of valproic acid (VPA) collected during routine clinical care of patients.”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, HT, AGE, BSA, DDVPA, SEX, Comedication (PHB or CBZ) Included: WT, DDVPA, PHB
raw.observations: Final regression model reduced inter-individual variability by 31%. A high residual variability may due to fixed Ka, F and Vd values, chosen from literature. Covariates found to influence VPA-PK are WT, DDVPA, and comedication with PHB. WT is evaluated as a better indicator of physiological conditions and metabolic capacity than age. DDVPA has linear relationship with VPA CL. PHB, a known enzyme inducer, enhances biotransformation of VPA. CBZ did not show significant influence on CL. Also gender did not improve fitting of the model. Limitation: Model only included comedication PHB and CBZ.
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.structure: one-compartment model with first-order absorption and elimination.
raw.equations: CL = ((theta1+(theta2*WT)+(theta3*DOSE))*(1+(theta4*PHB))) *exp(eta1) KA = theta5 V = theta6*WT Cobs=Cipred+eps1 WT: total body weight (kg) DOSE: daily dose of VPA (mg/day) PHB: comedication phenobarbital (1: yes, 0:no)
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 0.0466 theta2= 0.00363 theta3= 0.000282 theta4= 0.236 theta5=1.2 theta6=0.24 omega1= 0.02 sigma1=17.3

extraction_target.title: Population pharmacokinetics of valproate in Mexican children with epilepsy.

structural_model.description: one-compartment model with first-order absorption and elimination.

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL = ((theta1+(theta2*WT)+(theta3*DOSE))*(1+(theta4*PHB))) *exp(eta1) KA = theta5 V = theta6*WT Cobs=Cipred+eps1 WT: total body weight (kg) DOSE: daily dose of VPA (mg/day) PHB: comedication phenobarbital (1: yes, 0:no)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.objective: “to determine the factors that influence valproate clearance (CL) in Mexican epileptic pediatric patients using a mixed-effect model and sparse data of serum concentrations of valproic acid (VPA) collected during routine clinical care of patients.”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, HT, AGE, BSA, DDVPA, SEX, Comedication (PHB or CBZ) Included: WT, DDVPA, PHB

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
